prompt:
Use case: logo-brand
Asset type: fifth-round product app icon exploration for CopayGuard
Primary request: a happier, friendlier version of the dark orange developer-tool defensive pill-and-shield logo
Subject: simple pill-shaped mascot defending itself with a shield larger than its body; pill peeks from behind the large shield; one visible left hand grips the shield edge; mascot has a clear warm smile and calm friendly eyes; shield is protective, not aggressive
Style/medium: minimalist app icon, dark rounded-square background, geometric technical illustration, clean vector-friendly shapes, subtle pixel/block construction, premium developer-tool aesthetic, friendly and approachable
Mood: happy, helpful, warm, safe, confident; less severe than the previous pass
Composition/framing: centered inside a dark rounded square, shield dominates the mark but the smiling pill face is visible, large readable silhouette, app-icon-ready at small size
Color palette: near-black charcoal background, graphite gray pill and shield fills, warm orange/copper outlines and accents, muted amber highlights, soft cream highlights; brighter and warmer than the previous dark pass
Text (verbatim): none
Constraints: no written text, no letters, no numbers, no pseudo-code glyphs, no binary digits, no wordmark, no watermark, no photorealism, no red cross, no Claude logo, no Anthropic starburst, no asterisk/sunburst, no exact imitation of any existing brand mark
Avoid: teal, blue, green, purple, red, busy background, scary posture, angry eyebrows, detailed fingers, medical-cross symbol, copied Claude Code icon, fake text
Variation: smiling pill peeks from behind the left side of a large orange-rimmed shield, left hand gently bracing the rim, rounded friendly geometry, no technical text marks.
